title: Burnwatch Billing Manifest
description: Describes how a service charges, what's included in each plan, and what overage rates apply. Community contributors: add a new service by creating a <service-id>.json file in this directory following this schema.

serviceId.description: Must match the service ID in registry.json

lastVerified.description: When this pricing data was last verified against the service's pricing page (YYYY-MM-DD)

pricingUrl.description: URL to the service's pricing page for verification

notes.description: Freeform notes about this service's billing quirks

billingDimensions.description: Each dimension is a separate axis the service charges on (e.g., input tokens, output tokens, storage, bandwidth). Most services have 1-3 dimensions.

plans.description: Available plan tiers. Each plan specifies what's included and any overrides to default rates.

costMultipliers.description: Feature flags or usage patterns that multiply the base cost (e.g., anti-bot bypass on Scrapfly, long browser sessions on Browserbase). Used by cost-impact analysis.

typicalDevUsage.description: Typical usage patterns during development. Helps cost-impact make better projections than the generic '50 dev runs/month' default.

callsPerDevHour.description: How many API calls a developer typically makes per hour of active development

unitsPerCall.description: Average units consumed per API call (e.g., ~1000 tokens per LLM call, 1 credit per scrape)

unitName.description: What the unit is (tokens, credits, sessions, emails, etc.)

ratePerUnit.description: Default cost per unit in USD. For token-based services, this is per-token (not per million). For 'per million' pricing, divide by 1,000,000.

ratePer.description: If the rate is quoted 'per N units' (e.g., '$3 per 1M tokens'), set this to N (1000000) and ratePerUnit to the dollar amount ($3). The engine computes: cost = (units / ratePer) * ratePerUnit

variants.description: If the rate varies by model, feature, or tier (e.g., Haiku vs Opus), list each variant here. The engine uses the variant matching the detected model in code.

ratePerUnit.description: Rate for this variant in the same units as the parent dimension

codePatterns.description: Regex patterns that identify this variant in source code (e.g., 'claude-3-5-haiku', 'claude-3.5-haiku'). Used by cost-impact to pick the right rate.

isDefault.description: Use this variant's rate when no specific model is detected in code

name.description: Human-readable plan name (e.g., 'Pro ($25/mo)'). Must match registry.json plan names.

included.description: Units included in this plan before overages. Keys are billingDimension IDs, values are included amounts.

overageRates.description: Per-unit overage rates that override the default dimension rate. Keys are billingDimension IDs.

hardCap.description: If true, usage beyond plan limits is blocked rather than charged as overage

isDefault.description: Whether this is the most common plan tier

factor.description: Multiplier applied to base cost (e.g., 5 means 5x the base rate)

description.description: Explains when and why this multiplier applies

codePatterns.description: Regex patterns in source code that indicate this multiplier is active (e.g., 'antibot.*true', 'render_js.*true')
